- Behavior: lists certificate directories, types, ports, and usage based on nginx config and port mappings.

<a id="fn-remove_cert"></a>
### remove_cert (`lib/certs/remove_cert.sh`)
- Entry points: CLI `remove-cert`.
- Behavior: removes certificate directories only if no port mappings depend on them; certificate/archive/renewal file deletions are validated by safe-delete guards; updates nginx when removal succeeds.
- Side effects: deletes certificate directories, backups, `update_nginx_config`.

<a id="fn-renew_certs"></a>
### renew_certs (`lib/certs/renew_certs.sh`)
- Entry points: CLI `renew-certs`.
- Behavior: scans HTTPS port mappings, checks expiration, renews Let's Encrypt certificates in window, refreshes nginx config.

<a id="fn-_renew_letsencrypt_cert"></a>
### _renew_letsencrypt_cert (`lib/certs/_renew_letsencrypt_cert.sh`)
- Entry points: `renew_certs`.
- Behavior: runs certbot in webroot or standalone mode; pulls certbot image per policy; refreshes nginx config before webroot renewal.
- Side effects: runs Docker certbot, writes certificates under `CERTS_DIR`, may `update_nginx_config`.

<a id="fn-_cert_expiring_soon"></a>
### _cert_expiring_soon (`lib/certs/_cert_expiring_soon.sh`)
- Entry points: `renew_certs`.
- Behavior: checks if a certificate expires within a window.

<a id="fn-_notify_cert_warning"></a>
### _notify_cert_warning (`lib/certs/_notify_cert_warning.sh`)
- Entry points: `renew_certs`.
- Behavior: logs warnings and optionally emails via `mail` or `sendmail`.

<a id="fn-check_certbot_installed"></a>
### check_certbot_installed (`lib/certs/check_certbot_installed.sh`)
- Entry points: certificate creation/renewal.
- Behavior: no-op when using Docker-based certbot.

<a id="fn-cert_created_timestamp"></a>
### cert_created_timestamp (`lib/certs/cert_created_timestamp.sh`)
- Entry points: `list_certs`.
- Behavior: returns file mtime in a cross-platform way.

## Module: TLS (`lib/tls/*.sh`)

<a id="fn-set_tls_protocols"></a>
### set_tls_protocols (`lib/tls/set_tls_protocols.sh`)
- Entry points: CLI `set-tls-protocols`.
- Behavior: validates protocol list, stores global setting, updates nginx.

<a id="fn-set_tls_ciphers"></a>
### set_tls_ciphers (`lib/tls/set_tls_ciphers.sh`)
- Entry points: CLI `set-tls-ciphers`.
- Behavior: validates cipher string using `openssl ciphers`, stores global setting, updates nginx.

<a id="fn-set_port_tls_protocols"></a>
### set_port_tls_protocols (`lib/tls/set_port_tls_protocols.sh`)
- Entry points: CLI `set-port-tls-protocols`.
- Behavior: validates the HTTPS port mapping, stores per-port override, updates nginx.

<a id="fn-remove_port_tls_protocols"></a>
### remove_port_tls_protocols (`lib/tls/remove_port_tls_protocols.sh`)
- Entry points: CLI `remove-port-tls-protocols`.
- Behavior: removes per-port protocol overrides and updates nginx.

<a id="fn-set_port_tls_ciphers"></a>
### set_port_tls_ciphers (`lib/tls/set_port_tls_ciphers.sh`)
- Entry points: CLI `set-port-tls-ciphers`.
- Behavior: validates the HTTPS port mapping and cipher string, stores override, updates nginx.

<a id="fn-remove_port_tls_ciphers"></a>
### remove_port_tls_ciphers (`lib/tls/remove_port_tls_ciphers.sh`)
- Entry points: CLI `remove-port-tls-ciphers`.
- Behavior: removes per-port cipher overrides and updates nginx.

<a id="fn-get_port_tls_protocols"></a>
### get_port_tls_protocols (`lib/tls/common.sh`)
- Entry points: config generation and prompts.
- Behavior: returns per-port override or global default.

<a id="fn-get_port_tls_ciphers"></a>
### get_port_tls_ciphers (`lib/tls/common.sh`)
- Entry points: config generation and prompts.
- Behavior: returns per-port override or global default.

<a id="fn-_validate_tls_protocols"></a>
### _validate_tls_protocols (`lib/tls/common.sh`)
- Entry points: TLS setters.
- Behavior: validates TLS protocol tokens.

<a id="fn-_validate_tls_ciphers"></a>
### _validate_tls_ciphers (`lib/tls/common.sh`)
- Entry points: TLS setters.
- Behavior: validates cipher string via `openssl ciphers`.

<a id="fn-validate_tls_settings_for_render"></a>
### validate_tls_settings_for_render (`lib/tls/common.sh`)
- Entry points: `update_nginx_config`, `fix_default_config`.
- Behavior: fail-closed validation for persisted global and per-port TLS protocol/cipher state before config rendering.

<a id="fn-_require_https_port_mapping"></a>
### _require_https_port_mapping (`lib/tls/common.sh`)
- Entry points: per-port TLS setters.
- Behavior: ensures a port is mapped and uses HTTPS.

## Module: HTTP Version (`lib/http_version/*.sh`)

<a id="fn-set_http_version"></a>
### set_http_version (`lib/http_version/set_http_version.sh`)
- Entry points: CLI `set-http-version`.
- Behavior: sets global HTTP version, saves config, updates nginx.

<a id="fn-set_backend_http_version"></a>
### set_backend_http_version (`lib/http_version/set_backend_http_version.sh`)
- Entry points: CLI `set-backend-http-version`.
- Behavior: stores per-backend HTTP version override, updates nginx.
- Side effects: updates `BACKEND_HTTP_FILE`, backups, `update_nginx_config`.

<a id="fn-remove_backend_http_version"></a>
### remove_backend_http_version (`lib/http_version/remove_backend_http_version.sh`)
- Entry points: CLI `remove-backend-http-version`.
- Behavior: removes override for a backend.
- Side effects: updates `BACKEND_HTTP_FILE`, backups, `update_nginx_config`.

<a id="fn-get_backend_http_version"></a>
### get_backend_http_version (`lib/http_version/get_backend_http_version.sh`)
- Entry points: config generation and status.
- Behavior: returns per-backend override or global.

<a id="fn-get_proxy_http_version"></a>
### get_proxy_http_version (`lib/http_version/get_proxy_http_version.sh`)
- Entry points: config generation.
- Behavior: maps `http2` to `1.1` for upstream `proxy_http_version`.

## Module: headers (`lib/headers/*.sh`)

<a id="fn-set_header"></a>
### set_header (`lib/headers/set_header.sh`)
- Entry points: CLI `add-header` and `update-header`.
- Behavior: validates header name, type, and value (rejects control characters), stores global header entry.
- Side effects: updates `CUSTOM_HEADERS_FILE`, backups, `update_nginx_config`.

<a id="fn-remove_header"></a>
### remove_header (`lib/headers/remove_header.sh`)
- Entry points: CLI `remove-header`.
- Behavior: removes a global header entry.
- Side effects: updates `CUSTOM_HEADERS_FILE`, backups, `update_nginx_config`.

<a id="fn-list_headers"></a>
### list_headers (`lib/headers/list_headers.sh`)
- Entry points: CLI `list-headers`.
- Behavior: prints global headers.

<a id="fn-set_backend_header"></a>
### set_backend_header (`lib/headers/set_backend_header.sh`)
- Entry points: CLI `add-backend-header` and `update-backend-header`.
- Behavior: validates backend, header, and value (rejects control characters), stores per-backend header override.
- Side effects: updates `BACKEND_HEADERS_FILE`, backups, `update_nginx_config`.

<a id="fn-remove_backend_header"></a>
### remove_backend_header (`lib/headers/remove_backend_header.sh`)
- Entry points: CLI `remove-backend-header`.
- Behavior: removes a per-backend header override.
- Side effects: updates `BACKEND_HEADERS_FILE`, backups, `update_nginx_config`.

<a id="fn-list_backend_headers"></a>
### list_backend_headers (`lib/headers/list_backend_headers.sh`)
- Entry points: CLI `list-backend-headers`.
- Behavior: lists backend headers (all or for a domain).

<a id="fn-remove_all_headers"></a>
### remove_all_headers (`lib/headers/remove_all_headers.sh`)
- Entry points: CLI `remove-all-headers`.
- Behavior: clears global header list.
- Side effects: updates `CUSTOM_HEADERS_FILE`, backups, `update_nginx_config`.

<a id="fn-remove_all_backend_headers"></a>
### remove_all_backend_headers (`lib/headers/remove_all_backend_headers.sh`)
- Entry points: CLI `remove-all-backend-headers`.
- Behavior: clears backend header overrides (all or for a domain).
- Side effects: updates `BACKEND_HEADERS_FILE`, backups, `update_nginx_config`.

<a id="fn-get_global_header_value"></a>
### get_global_header_value (`lib/headers/common.sh`)
- Entry points: interactive defaults.
- Behavior: returns a header value from `CUSTOM_HEADERS_FILE`.

<a id="fn-get_backend_header_value"></a>
### get_backend_header_value (`lib/headers/common.sh`)
- Entry points: interactive defaults.
- Behavior: returns a backend header override value.

<a id="fn-set_hsts"></a>
### set_hsts (`lib/headers/set_hsts.sh`)
- Entry points: CLI `set-hsts`.
- Behavior: sets or removes global HSTS header.

<a id="fn-set_backend_hsts"></a>
### set_backend_hsts (`lib/headers/set_backend_hsts.sh`)
- Entry points: CLI `set-backend-hsts`.
- Behavior: sets or removes backend-specific HSTS header.

<a id="fn-set_csp"></a>
### set_csp (`lib/headers/set_csp.sh`)
- Entry points: CLI `set-csp`.
- Behavior: sets or removes global CSP header.

<a id="fn-set_backend_csp"></a>
### set_backend_csp (`lib/headers/set_backend_csp.sh`)
- Entry points: CLI `set-backend-csp`.
- Behavior: sets or removes backend-specific CSP header.

<a id="fn-set_client_ip_header"></a>
### set_client_ip_header (`lib/headers/set_client_ip_header.sh`)
- Entry points: CLI `set-client-ip-header`.
- Behavior: sets global client IP header (or disables), saves config, updates nginx; `X-Forwarded-For` uses chain semantics and other header names forward the trusted client address via `$remote_addr`.

<a id="fn-set_proxy_ip_header"></a>
### set_proxy_ip_header (`lib/headers/set_proxy_ip_header.sh`)
- Entry points: CLI `set-proxy-ip-header`.
- Behavior: sets global proxy IP header (or disables), saves config, updates nginx.

<a id="fn-set_backend_client_ip_header"></a>
### set_backend_client_ip_header (`lib/headers/set_backend_client_ip_header.sh`)
- Entry points: CLI `set-backend-client-ip-header`.
- Behavior: sets per-backend client IP header override or disables.
- Side effects: updates `BACKEND_CLIENT_IP_HEADER_FILE`, backups, `update_nginx_config`.

<a id="fn-remove_backend_client_ip_header"></a>
### remove_backend_client_ip_header (`lib/headers/remove_backend_client_ip_header.sh`)
- Entry points: CLI `remove-backend-client-ip-header`.
- Behavior: removes per-backend client IP header override.
- Side effects: updates `BACKEND_CLIENT_IP_HEADER_FILE`, backups, `update_nginx_config`.

<a id="fn-set_backend_proxy_ip_header"></a>
### set_backend_proxy_ip_header (`lib/headers/set_backend_proxy_ip_header.sh`)
- Entry points: CLI `set-backend-proxy-ip-header`.
- Behavior: sets per-backend proxy IP header override or disables.
- Side effects: updates `BACKEND_PROXY_IP_HEADER_FILE`, backups, `update_nginx_config`.

<a id="fn-remove_backend_proxy_ip_header"></a>
### remove_backend_proxy_ip_header (`lib/headers/remove_backend_proxy_ip_header.sh`)
- Entry points: CLI `remove-backend-proxy-ip-header`.
- Behavior: removes per-backend proxy IP header override.
- Side effects: updates `BACKEND_PROXY_IP_HEADER_FILE`, backups, `update_nginx_config`.

<a id="fn-get_backend_client_ip_header"></a>
### get_backend_client_ip_header (`lib/headers/common.sh`)
- Entry points: nginx config generation.
- Behavior: returns per-backend client IP header override or global default.

<a id="fn-get_backend_proxy_ip_header"></a>
### get_backend_proxy_ip_header (`lib/headers/common.sh`)
- Entry points: nginx config generation.
- Behavior: returns per-backend proxy IP header override or global default.

<a id="fn-_build_header_files"></a>
### _build_header_files (`lib/headers/_build_header_files.sh`)
- Entry points: `update_nginx_config`.
- Behavior: validates persisted header type/domain/name/value rows, then generates Nginx include files for global and backend headers plus backend header maps keyed by the server-local backend identity variable.

<a id="fn-_escape_header_value"></a>
### _escape_header_value (`lib/headers/common.sh`)
- Entry points: header file generation.
- Behavior: escapes backslashes and quotes for Nginx config values.

<a id="fn-_validate_persisted_header_row"></a>
### _validate_persisted_header_row (`lib/headers/common.sh`)
- Entry points: `_build_header_files`.
- Behavior: fail-closed validation for persisted global/backend header rows before config rendering.

## Module: security_rules (`lib/security_rules/*.sh`)

<a id="fn-add_security_rule"></a>
### add_security_rule (`lib/security_rules/add_security_rule.sh`)
- Entry points: CLI `add-security-rule`.
- Inputs: domain, count or mode, condition tuples (source, name, condition, value), optional `--mode` and `--code`.
- Behavior: validates domain, selector names, and operators, rejects CIDR values for `ip|ip:l7|ip:l3` selectors (`equals`, `not_equals`, `in`, `not_in`), enforces numeric comparisons only for `var:` selectors, writes escaped rule line.
- Side effects: appends to `SECURITY_RULES_DB`, backups, `update_nginx_config`.

<a id="fn-update_security_rule"></a>
### update_security_rule (`lib/security_rules/update_security_rule.sh`)
- Entry points: CLI `update-security-rule`.
- Behavior: loads rule by ID, updates domain/mode/code/count and condition tuples, validates selector names and operators, rejects CIDR values for `ip|ip:l7|ip:l3` selectors, rewrites line.
- Side effects: updates `SECURITY_RULES_DB`, backups, `update_nginx_config`.

<a id="fn-set_security_rule_mode"></a>
### set_security_rule_mode (`lib/security_rules/set_security_rule_mode.sh`)
- Entry points: CLI `set-security-rule-mode`.
- Behavior: changes mode for multi-condition rules only.
- Side effects: updates `SECURITY_RULES_DB`, backups, `update_nginx_config`.

<a id="fn-list_security_rules"></a>
### list_security_rules (`lib/security_rules/list_security_rules.sh`)
- Entry points: CLI `list-security-rules`, `status`, `status-all`.
- Behavior: prints rules with IDs and conditions; supports domain filtering.

<a id="fn-remove_security_rule"></a>
### remove_security_rule (`lib/security_rules/remove_security_rule.sh`)
- Entry points: CLI `remove-security-rule`.
- Behavior: deletes a rule by line number.
- Side effects: updates `SECURITY_RULES_DB`, backups, `update_nginx_config`.

<a id="fn-remove_all_security_rules"></a>
### remove_all_security_rules (`lib/security_rules/remove_all_security_rules.sh`)
- Entry points: CLI `remove-all-security-rules`.
- Behavior: clears rule DB.
- Side effects: updates `SECURITY_RULES_DB`, backups, `update_nginx_config`.

<a id="fn-enable_security_rule"></a>
### enable_security_rule (`lib/security_rules/enable_security_rule.sh`)
- Entry points: CLI `enable-security-rule`.
- Behavior: sets enabled flag to `1` for the target rule line.
- Side effects: `create_backup` and `update_nginx_config`.

<a id="fn-disable_security_rule"></a>
### disable_security_rule (`lib/security_rules/disable_security_rule.sh`)
- Entry points: CLI `disable-security-rule`.
- Behavior: sets enabled flag to `0` for the target rule line.
- Side effects: `create_backup` and `update_nginx_config`.

<a id="fn-enable_all_security_rules"></a>
### enable_all_security_rules (`lib/security_rules/enable_all_security_rules.sh`)
- Entry points: CLI `enable-all-security-rules`.
- Behavior: sets enabled flag to `1` for all rule lines.
- Side effects: `create_backup` and `update_nginx_config`.

<a id="fn-disable_all_security_rules"></a>
### disable_all_security_rules (`lib/security_rules/disable_all_security_rules.sh`)
- Entry points: CLI `disable-all-security-rules`.
- Behavior: sets enabled flag to `0` for all rule lines.
- Side effects: `create_backup` and `update_nginx_config`.

<a id="fn-duplicate_security_rule"></a>
### duplicate_security_rule (`lib/security_rules/duplicate_security_rule.sh`)
- Entry points: CLI `duplicate-security-rule`.
- Behavior: appends a copy of an existing rule and reports new ID.
- Side effects: appends to `SECURITY_RULES_DB`, backups, `update_nginx_config`.

<a id="fn-move_security_rule"></a>
### move_security_rule (`lib/security_rules/move_security_rule.sh`)
- Entry points: CLI `move-security-rule`.
- Behavior: reorders rules by line number.
- Side effects: reorders `SECURITY_RULES_DB`, backups, `update_nginx_config`.

<a id="fn-build_security_rules_inc"></a>
### build_security_rules_inc (`lib/security_rules/build_security_rules_inc.sh`)
- Entry points: `update_nginx_config`.
- Behavior: regenerates `security_rules.inc` from DB and per-domain ACL includes; fails config generation when persisted security-rule rows contain invalid selector names or CIDR values for `ip|ip:l7|ip:l3` selectors.

<a id="fn-build_security_ip_includes"></a>
### build_security_ip_includes (`lib/security_rules/build_security_ip_includes.sh`)
- Entry points: `build_security_rules_inc`.
- Behavior: writes per-domain HTTP and stream include files from IP rules.

<a id="fn-_build_security_ip_for_domain"></a>
### _build_security_ip_for_domain (`lib/security_rules/_sr_builders.sh`)
- Entry points: `build_security_ip_includes`.
- Behavior: emits allow/deny directives and conditional returns for L7/L3 ACL policies and status codes (including `both` scope).

<a id="fn-_build_security_ip_stream_for_domain"></a>
### _build_security_ip_stream_for_domain (`lib/security_rules/_sr_builders.sh`)
- Entry points: `build_security_ip_includes`.
- Behavior: emits stream `allow`/`deny` directives for ACL rules (L7/L3 scopes map to client IP; status codes ignored).

<a id="fn-add_security_ip"></a>
### add_security_ip (`lib/security_rules/add_security_ip.sh`)
- Entry points: CLI `add-acl` (aliased).
- Inputs: domain, scope (l7|l3|both), action (allow|deny), IPs, optional status code.
- Behavior: validates domain/IP, pre-validates all tokens before writing, rejects CIDR values for `l3` and `both` scopes, rejects non-`403` status overrides for L7 CIDR deny rules, rejects exact duplicate ACL rows (same domain/scope/action/ip/code), then appends rule(s) to `SECURITY_IP_RULES_DB`.
- Side effects: updates `SECURITY_IP_RULES_DB`, backups, `update_nginx_config`.

<a id="fn-update_security_ip"></a>
### update_security_ip (`lib/security_rules/update_security_ip.sh`)
- Entry points: CLI `update-acl` (aliased).
- Behavior: updates a security IP rule by ID, validates scope/action/IP/code (l7|l3|both), rejects CIDR values for `l3` and `both`, and rejects final L7 CIDR deny rules with non-`403` status overrides.
- Side effects: updates `SECURITY_IP_RULES_DB`, backups, `update_nginx_config`.

<a id="fn-list_security_ip"></a>
### list_security_ip (`lib/security_rules/list_security_ip.sh`)
- Entry points: CLI `list-acl` (aliased), `status`, `status-all`.
- Behavior: lists IP rules and indicates disabled status.

<a id="fn-remove_security_ip"></a>
### remove_security_ip (`lib/security_rules/remove_security_ip.sh`)
- Entry points: CLI `remove-acl` (aliased).
- Behavior: removes IP rule by ID.
- Side effects: updates `SECURITY_IP_RULES_DB`, backups, `update_nginx_config`.

<a id="fn-enable_security_ip_rule"></a>
### enable_security_ip_rule (`lib/security_rules/enable_security_ip_rule.sh`)
- Entry points: CLI `enable-acl` (aliased).
- Behavior: removes the leading comment marker from the rule line.
- Side effects: `create_backup` and `update_nginx_config`.

<a id="fn-disable_security_ip_rule"></a>
### disable_security_ip_rule (`lib/security_rules/disable_security_ip_rule.sh`)
- Entry points: CLI `disable-acl` (aliased).
- Behavior: prefixes the rule line with `#` to disable it.
- Side effects: `create_backup` and `update_nginx_config`.

<a id="fn-enable_all_security_ip_rules"></a>
### enable_all_security_ip_rules (`lib/security_rules/enable_all_security_ip_rules.sh`)
- Entry points: CLI `enable-all-acl` (aliased).
- Behavior: removes leading comment markers from all rule lines.
- Side effects: `create_backup` and `update_nginx_config`.

<a id="fn-disable_all_security_ip_rules"></a>
### disable_all_security_ip_rules (`lib/security_rules/disable_all_security_ip_rules.sh`)
- Entry points: CLI `disable-all-acl` (aliased).
- Behavior: prefixes all non-empty, non-comment lines with `#`.
- Side effects: `create_backup` and `update_nginx_config`.

<a id="fn-remove_all_security_ip_rules"></a>
### remove_all_security_ip_rules (`lib/security_rules/remove_all_security_ip_rules.sh`)
- Entry points: CLI `remove-all-acl` (aliased).
- Behavior: clears the IP rules file.
- Side effects: updates `SECURITY_IP_RULES_DB`, backups, `update_nginx_config`.

<a id="fn-move_security_ip_rule"></a>
### move_security_ip_rule (`lib/security_rules/move_security_ip_rule.sh`)
- Entry points: CLI `move-acl-rule` (aliased).
- Behavior: reorders IP rules by line number.
- Side effects: reorders `SECURITY_IP_RULES_DB`, backups, `update_nginx_config`.

<a id="fn-set_acl_policy"></a>
### set_acl_policy (`lib/security_rules/set_acl_policy.sh`)
- Entry points: CLI `set-acl-policy`.
- Behavior: sets global allow/deny policy and updates nginx; blocks `deny` when effective ACL status is non-`403` and CIDR allow ACL rules are present.

<a id="fn-get_backend_acl_policy"></a>
### get_backend_acl_policy (`lib/security_rules/get_backend_acl_policy.sh`)
- Entry points: config generation and prompts.
- Behavior: returns per-backend ACL policy override or global.

<a id="fn-set_backend_acl_policy"></a>
### set_backend_acl_policy (`lib/security_rules/set_backend_acl_policy.sh`)
- Entry points: CLI `set-backend-acl-policy`.
- Behavior: stores per-backend ACL policy override; blocks `deny` when effective ACL status is non-`403` and CIDR allow ACL rules are present, including inheriting dedicated hosts mapped to that backend.
- Side effects: updates `BACKEND_ACL_POLICY_FILE`, backups, `update_nginx_config`.

<a id="fn-remove_backend_acl_policy"></a>
### remove_backend_acl_policy (`lib/security_rules/remove_backend_acl_policy.sh`)
- Entry points: CLI `remove-backend-acl-policy`.
- Behavior: removes per-backend ACL policy override.
- Side effects: updates `BACKEND_ACL_POLICY_FILE`, backups, `update_nginx_config`.

<a id="fn-get_backend_acl_status"></a>
### get_backend_acl_status (`lib/security_rules/get_backend_acl_status.sh`)
- Entry points: config generation and prompts.
- Behavior: returns per-backend ACL status override or global.

<a id="fn-set_backend_acl_status"></a>
### set_backend_acl_status (`lib/security_rules/set_backend_acl_status.sh`)
- Entry points: CLI `set-backend-acl-status`.
- Behavior: stores per-backend ACL status override; blocks non-`403` codes when effective ACL policy is `deny` and CIDR allow ACL rules are present, including inheriting dedicated hosts mapped to that backend.
- Side effects: updates `BACKEND_ACL_STATUS_FILE`, backups, `update_nginx_config`.

<a id="fn-remove_backend_acl_status"></a>
### remove_backend_acl_status (`lib/security_rules/remove_backend_acl_status.sh`)
- Entry points: CLI `remove-backend-acl-status`.
- Behavior: removes per-backend ACL status override.
- Side effects: updates `BACKEND_ACL_STATUS_FILE`, backups, `update_nginx_config`.

<a id="fn-get_backend_security_rule_status"></a>
### get_backend_security_rule_status (`lib/security_rules/get_backend_security_rule_status.sh`)
- Entry points: config generation and prompts.
- Behavior: returns per-backend security rule status override or global.

<a id="fn-set_backend_security_rule_status"></a>
### set_backend_security_rule_status (`lib/security_rules/set_backend_security_rule_status.sh`)
- Entry points: CLI `set-backend-security-rule-status`.
- Behavior: stores per-backend security rule status override.
- Side effects: updates `BACKEND_SECURITY_RULE_STATUS_FILE`, backups, `update_nginx_config`.

<a id="fn-remove_backend_security_rule_status"></a>
### remove_backend_security_rule_status (`lib/security_rules/remove_backend_security_rule_status.sh`)
- Entry points: CLI `remove-backend-security-rule-status`.
- Behavior: removes per-backend security rule status override.
- Side effects: updates `BACKEND_SECURITY_RULE_STATUS_FILE`, backups, `update_nginx_config`.

<a id="fn-condition_alias"></a>
### condition_alias (`lib/security_rules/_sr_strings.sh`)
- Entry points: interactive prompts.
- Behavior: returns title-cased label for condition names.

<a id="fn-_sr_escape"></a>
### _sr_escape (`lib/security_rules/_sr_strings.sh`)
- Entry points: DB write helpers.
- Behavior: escapes user input for safe Nginx expression construction and regex generation.

<a id="fn-_sr_unescape"></a>
### _sr_unescape (`lib/security_rules/_sr_strings.sh`)
- Entry points: DB read helpers.
- Behavior: decodes escaped selector/value fragments used during rule-expression generation.

<a id="fn-_sr_write_db_line"></a>
### _sr_write_db_line (`lib/security_rules/_sr_db.sh`)
- Entry points: rule creation/update.
- Behavior: assembles a schema-aligned CSV row for the security-rules DB.

<a id="fn-_sr_load_line"></a>
### _sr_load_line (`lib/security_rules/_sr_db.sh`)
- Entry points: rule update/enable/disable.
- Behavior: loads a rule line by line number.

<a id="fn-_sr_delete_line"></a>
### _sr_delete_line (`lib/security_rules/_sr_db.sh`)
- Entry points: rule removal.
- Behavior: deletes a line from DB.

<a id="fn-_sr_replace_line"></a>
### _sr_replace_line (`lib/security_rules/_sr_db.sh`)
- Entry points: rule update and toggles.
- Behavior: replaces a line by line number.

<a id="fn-_sr_source_to_selector"></a>
### _sr_source_to_selector (`lib/security_rules/_sr_selectors.sh`)
- Entry points: rule validation and generation.
- Behavior: maps source + optional name to a selector string.

<a id="fn-_sr_selector_to_var"></a>
### _sr_selector_to_var (`lib/security_rules/_sr_selectors.sh`)
- Entry points: rule generation.
- Behavior: maps selector to Nginx variable and label.

<a id="fn-_sr_allowed_ops_for_selector"></a>
### _sr_allowed_ops_for_selector (`lib/security_rules/_sr_selectors.sh`)
- Entry points: validation and interactive filtering.
- Behavior: returns allowed conditions per selector type.

<a id="fn-_sr_validate_selector_condition"></a>
### _sr_validate_selector_condition (`lib/security_rules/_sr_selectors.sh`)
- Entry points: rule validation.
- Behavior: errors when condition not allowed for selector.

<a id="fn-_sr_validate_selector_name"></a>
### _sr_validate_selector_name (`lib/security_rules/common.sh`)
- Entry points: rule validation.
- Behavior: validates `header`, `cookie`, `arg`, and `var` selector names before save or persisted render.

<a id="fn-_sr_validate_acl_cidr_deny_status"></a>
### _sr_validate_acl_cidr_deny_status (`lib/security_rules/common.sh`)
- Entry points: `add_security_ip`, `update_security_ip`.
- Behavior: rejects L7 CIDR deny ACL rules with non-`403` status overrides because Nginx CIDR `deny` rules always return `403`.

<a id="fn-_sr_exprs"></a>
### _sr_exprs (`lib/security_rules/_sr_selectors.sh`)
- Entry points: rule generation.
- Behavior: returns Nginx predicate fragments for a condition and value.

<a id="fn-_sr_next_rule_var"></a>
### _sr_next_rule_var (`lib/security_rules/_sr_db.sh`)
- Entry points: rule generation.
- Behavior: increments a counter and provides a unique variable prefix.

<a id="fn-_sr_numeric_inc"></a>
### _sr_numeric_inc (`lib/security_rules/_sr_numeric.sh`)
- Entry points: numeric comparisons.
- Behavior: increments a numeric string.

<a id="fn-_sr_numeric_regex_ge"></a>
### _sr_numeric_regex_ge (`lib/security_rules/_sr_numeric.sh`)
- Entry points: numeric comparisons.
- Behavior: builds regex that matches numbers >= input.

<a id="fn-_sr_numeric_regex_ge_same"></a>
### _sr_numeric_regex_ge_same (`lib/security_rules/_sr_numeric.sh`)
- Entry points: numeric comparisons.
- Behavior: helper to build regex for same-length numbers >= input.

<a id="fn-_sr_trim_leading_zeros"></a>
### _sr_trim_leading_zeros (`lib/security_rules/_sr_numeric.sh`)
- Entry points: numeric comparisons.
- Behavior: strips leading zeros.

<a id="fn-_sr_is_unsigned_int"></a>
### _sr_is_unsigned_int (`lib/security_rules/_sr_numeric.sh`)
- Entry points: numeric comparisons.
- Behavior: validates unsigned integer.

<a id="fn-_sr_escape_regex_literal"></a>
### _sr_escape_regex_literal (`lib/security_rules/_sr_strings.sh`)
- Entry points: regex condition building.
- Behavior: escapes regex metacharacters.

<a id="fn-_sr_assert_domain_exists"></a>
### _sr_assert_domain_exists (`lib/security_rules/_sr_db.sh`)
- Entry points: rule creation/update.
- Behavior: validates that a backend exists for a domain.

<a id="fn-_escape_nginx_value"></a>
### _escape_nginx_value (`lib/security_rules/_sr_strings.sh`)
- Entry points: rule generation.
- Behavior: escapes quotes and backslashes for Nginx configs.

<a id="fn-_generate_security_rule_line"></a>
### _generate_security_rule_line (`lib/security_rules/_sr_builders.sh`)
- Entry points: `build_security_rules_inc`.
- Behavior: emits a single-condition Nginx rule line.

<a id="fn-_generate_security_rule_multi_line"></a>
### _generate_security_rule_multi_line (`lib/security_rules/_sr_builders.sh`)
- Entry points: `build_security_rules_inc`.
- Behavior: emits multi-condition rule logic that denies `or` rules only when all predicates fail and denies `and` rules whenever any predicate fails.

<a id="fn-_is_ip"></a>
### _is_ip (`lib/security_rules/_sr_builders.sh`)
- Entry points: ACL policy generation.
- Behavior: detects IPv4 dotted-quad patterns.

## Module: mtls (`lib/mtls/*.sh`)

<a id="fn-enable_backend_mtls"></a>
### enable_backend_mtls (`lib/mtls/enable_backend_mtls.sh`)
- Entry points: CLI `enable-backend-mtls`.
- Behavior: validates the domain and known backend before any filesystem mutation, normalizes the target path under `CERTS_DIR/mtls`, creates CA if missing, initializes mTLS (mutual TLS) state, stores domain->dir mapping, optionally issues a client certificate, generates CRL, updates nginx.
- Side effects: creates files under `CERTS_DIR/mtls/<domain>` and updates `BACKEND_MTLS_FILE`.

<a id="fn-disable_backend_mtls"></a>
### disable_backend_mtls (`lib/mtls/disable_backend_mtls.sh`)
- Entry points: CLI `disable-backend-mtls`.
- Behavior: validates and normalizes the domain, removes the matching domain mapping from `BACKEND_MTLS_FILE`, and updates nginx.

<a id="fn-add_backend_client_cert"></a>
### add_backend_client_cert (`lib/mtls/add_backend_client_cert.sh`)
- Entry points: CLI `add-backend-client-cert`.
- Behavior: validates the domain and client name, resolves a contained mTLS directory for the backend, issues a client certificate signed by backend CA, updates CRL.
- Side effects: writes client certificate files/CRL, `update_nginx_config`.

<a id="fn-remove_backend_client_cert"></a>
### remove_backend_client_cert (`lib/mtls/remove_backend_client_cert.sh`)
- Entry points: CLI `remove-backend-client-cert`.
- Behavior: validates the domain and client name, resolves a contained mTLS directory, revokes a certificate, deletes client files.
- Side effects: updates CRL and client files, `update_nginx_config`.

<a id="fn-revoke_backend_client_cert"></a>
### revoke_backend_client_cert (`lib/mtls/revoke_backend_client_cert.sh`)
- Entry points: CLI `revoke-backend-client-cert`.
- Behavior: validates the domain and client name, resolves a contained mTLS directory, revokes a certificate but keeps files.
- Side effects: updates CRL, `update_nginx_config`.

<a id="fn-replace_backend_client_cert"></a>
### replace_backend_client_cert (`lib/mtls/replace_backend_client_cert.sh`)
- Entry points: CLI `replace-backend-client-cert`.
- Behavior: validates the domain and client name, resolves a contained mTLS directory, removes and re-issues a client certificate.
- Side effects: updates client certificate files/CRL, `update_nginx_config`.

<a id="fn-export_backend_client_p12"></a>
### export_backend_client_p12 (`lib/mtls/export_backend_client_p12.sh`)
- Entry points: CLI `export-backend-client-p12`.
- Behavior: validates the domain and client name, resolves a contained mTLS directory, validates `--password-env` names before indirect expansion, exports PKCS#12 bundle with the user-supplied password source.

<a id="fn-list_backend_client_certs"></a>
### list_backend_client_certs (`lib/mtls/list_backend_client_certs.sh`)
- Entry points: CLI `list-backend-client-certs`.
- Behavior: validates the domain, revalidates the persisted mTLS directory stays under `CERTS_DIR/mtls`, then lists client certificates and their status based on `index.txt`.

<a id="fn-list_backend_cas"></a>
### list_backend_cas (`lib/mtls/list_backend_cas.sh`)
- Entry points: CLI `list-backend-cas`.
- Behavior: validates persisted mTLS directories stay under `CERTS_DIR/mtls`, then lists backend CAs and expiration dates; invalid persisted paths are reported without revealing or inspecting the raw path.

<a id="fn-replace_backend_ca"></a>
### replace_backend_ca (`lib/mtls/replace_backend_ca.sh`)
- Entry points: CLI `replace-backend-ca`.
- Behavior: validates the domain, revalidates the persisted mTLS directory stays under `CERTS_DIR/mtls`, regenerates CA and removes client certificates.
- Side effects: rewrites CA material/CRL, `update_nginx_config`.

<a id="fn-remove_backend_ca"></a>
### remove_backend_ca (`lib/mtls/remove_backend_ca.sh`)
- Entry points: CLI `remove-backend-ca`.
- Behavior: validates the domain, revalidates the persisted mTLS directory stays under `CERTS_DIR/mtls`, disables mTLS, and deletes the CA directory through safe-delete guards.
- Side effects: removes CA/client files, `update_nginx_config`.

<a id="fn-get_backend_mtls_dir"></a>
### get_backend_mtls_dir (`lib/mtls/get_backend_mtls_dir.sh`)
- Entry points: mTLS commands and nginx config.
- Behavior: looks up stored mTLS directory for a backend.

<a id="fn-_init_backend_mtls_state"></a>
### _init_backend_mtls_state (`lib/mtls/_init_backend_mtls_state.sh`)
- Entry points: mTLS setup and certificate issuance.
- Behavior: initializes CA database files and openssl config.

<a id="fn-_write_backend_openssl_conf"></a>
### _write_backend_openssl_conf (`lib/mtls/_write_backend_openssl_conf.sh`)
- Entry points: mTLS helpers.
- Behavior: writes openssl.cnf for CA operations.

<a id="fn-_issue_backend_client_cert"></a>
### _issue_backend_client_cert (`lib/mtls/_issue_backend_client_cert.sh`)
- Entry points: add/enable mTLS.
- Behavior: creates CSR, signs it with backend CA, stores key/crt.

<a id="fn-_revoke_backend_client_cert"></a>
### _revoke_backend_client_cert (`lib/mtls/_revoke_backend_client_cert.sh`)
- Entry points: revoke/remove client certificate.
- Behavior: revokes a certificate in the CA db, updates CRL, optionally deletes files.

<a id="fn-_generate_backend_crl"></a>
### _generate_backend_crl (`lib/mtls/_generate_backend_crl.sh`)
- Entry points: mTLS setup and revocation.
- Behavior: writes CRL file.

## Module: backups (`lib/backups/*.sh`)

<a id="fn-create_backup"></a>
### create_backup (`lib/backups/create_backup.sh`)
- Entry points: most mutating commands.
- Behavior: creates backup directories or tar archives; uses checksums to avoid redundant backups; enforces retention.
- Side effects: writes under `BACKUP_DIR`, updates `FULL_BACKUP_*` markers.

<a id="fn-create_backup_cmd"></a>
### create_backup_cmd (`lib/backups/create_backup_cmd.sh`)
- Entry points: CLI `create-backup`.
- Behavior: forces a manual backup with a description.

<a id="fn-list_backups"></a>
### list_backups (`lib/backups/list_backups.sh`)
- Entry points: CLI `list-backups`.
- Behavior: lists backup archives and directories.

<a id="fn-restore_backup"></a>
### restore_backup (`lib/backups/restore_backup.sh`)
- Entry points: CLI `restore-backup`.
- Behavior: restores `CONFIG_DIR` from either a backup name under `BACKUP_DIR` or an explicit backup path (directory or tar.gz) in place (preserving the `CONFIG_DIR` inode), wraps changes in a transaction, fails fast if existing config cleanup cannot complete, normalizes restored permissions, regenerates nginx config, recreates the Nginx container when it was running pre-restore to refresh bind mounts, and validates config with `check-config` when Docker checks are enabled. On rollback, restore transactions clear restored content before replaying the pre-change archive so backup-only files are removed. Archive restores require safe paths (no absolute or `..` segments) and a single top-level directory.
- Interactive notes: prompts for confirmation unless non-interactive.

<a id="fn-backup_files_only"></a>
### backup_files_only (`lib/backups/backup_files_only.sh`)
- Entry points: transactions (pre/post).
- Behavior: creates a tar.gz containing only given files relative to `STATE_DIR`.

<a id="fn-restore_backup_archive"></a>
### restore_backup_archive (`lib/backups/restore_backup_archive.sh`)
- Entry points: rollback handler.
- Behavior: extracts a transaction archive into `STATE_DIR` after validating archive paths.

<a id="fn-begin_transaction"></a>
### begin_transaction (`lib/backups/begin_transaction.sh`)
- Entry points: mutating commands that need rollback.
- Behavior: acquires a fail-fast transaction lock under `TMP_DIR`, treats permission-denied PID probes as active ownership, retries owner metadata reads to avoid lock-acquire races, reclaims ownerless locks only when stale, supports nested begin/end depth semantics only for the same owning process, captures pre-change archive on the outermost transaction, sets rollback variables, and installs ERR trap.

<a id="fn-end_transaction_success"></a>
### end_transaction_success (`lib/backups/end_transaction_success.sh`)
- Entry points: successful transactional flows.
- Behavior: decrements nested transaction depth; on outermost success captures post-change archive, updates checksum markers, clears rollback/owner state, and releases transaction lock.

<a id="fn-_rollback_handler"></a>
### _rollback_handler (`lib/backups/_rollback_handler.sh`)
- Entry points: ERR trap.
- Behavior: runs optional rollback pre-hook, captures container logs, clears restore targets safely, restores pre-change archive, removes newly-created paths safely, clears transaction and owner state, releases transaction lock, logs error, exits.

<a id="fn-capture_docker_logs"></a>
### capture_docker_logs (`lib/backups/capture_docker_logs.sh`)
- Entry points: rollback handler.
- Behavior: saves Docker logs to `ERROR_LOG_DIR`.

<a id="fn-_config_checksum"></a>
### _config_checksum (`lib/backups/_config_checksum.sh`)
- Entry points: backup and transaction logic.
- Behavior: computes a SHA1 checksum of config directory contents.

<a id="fn-is_transaction_backup_archive"></a>
### is_transaction_backup_archive (`lib/backups/common.sh`)
- Entry points: transaction logic.
- Behavior: detects pre/post transaction archives by name and content.

## Module: access_log (`lib/access_log/*.sh`)

<a id="fn-_ensure_log_fields_file"></a>
### _ensure_log_fields_file (`lib/access_log/_ensure_log_fields_file.sh`)
- Entry points: `load_config`, log field operations.
- Behavior: creates default log field list if missing.

<a id="fn-list_log_fields"></a>
### list_log_fields (`lib/access_log/list_log_fields.sh`)
- Entry points: CLI `list-log-fields`, `status-all`.
- Behavior: prints log fields with indices and request/response hints.

<a id="fn-add_log_field"></a>
### add_log_field (`lib/access_log/add_log_field.sh`)
- Entry points: CLI `add-log-field`.
- Behavior: inserts or appends a log field and refreshes nginx config.
- Side effects: updates `ACCESS_LOG_FIELDS_FILE`, backups, regenerates `nginx.conf`, `update_nginx_config`.

<a id="fn-remove_log_field"></a>
### remove_log_field (`lib/access_log/remove_log_field.sh`)
- Entry points: CLI `remove-log-field`.
- Behavior: deletes a log field by line number.
- Side effects: updates `ACCESS_LOG_FIELDS_FILE`, backups, regenerates `nginx.conf`, `update_nginx_config`.

<a id="fn-update_log_field"></a>
### update_log_field (`lib/access_log/update_log_field.sh`)
- Entry points: CLI `update-log-field`.
- Behavior: replaces a log field by ID.
- Side effects: updates `ACCESS_LOG_FIELDS_FILE`, backups, regenerates `nginx.conf`, `update_nginx_config`.

<a id="fn-move_log_field"></a>
### move_log_field (`lib/access_log/move_log_field.sh`)
- Entry points: CLI `move-log-field`.
- Behavior: reorders log fields.
- Side effects: updates `ACCESS_LOG_FIELDS_FILE`, backups, regenerates `nginx.conf`, `update_nginx_config`.

## Module: capture (`lib/capture/*.sh`)

<a id="fn-start_capture"></a>
### start_capture (`lib/capture/start_capture.sh`)
- Entry points: CLI `start-capture`.
- Inputs: folder, scope, backends, clients, optional `--tls-decrypt`.
- Behavior: validates the digest-pinned capture image reference, validates `--clients` tokens as IPv4 literals and rejects invalid client filters before Docker capture startup, builds tcpdump filters based on backend ports and client IPs; normalizes relative output folders under `state/pcaps` (including `pcaps/...` and `state/pcaps/...` aliases); rejects traversal/absolute output paths that resolve outside `state/pcaps`; runs the selected capture image container attached to nginx network namespace; optional TLS decrypt mode is enabled explicitly via `--tls-decrypt` or interactive prompt confirmation.
- Side effects: starts `nginx-capture` container, writes `.pcap` in output dir, and when decrypt mode is enabled writes per-session keylog state/files (directory/file modes `700/600`), builds the TLS keylog helper on demand inside the configured Nginx image, and recreates nginx with the helper mounted through `LD_PRELOAD` when Docker checks are enabled.
- Interactive notes: prompts for TLS decrypt enablement (default `No`), scope, and targets.

<a id="fn-stop_capture"></a>
### stop_capture (`lib/capture/stop_capture.sh`)
- Entry points: CLI `stop-capture`.
- Behavior: stops and removes `nginx-capture` container and attached anonymous volumes if present, disables TLS decrypt capture mode, preserves keylog artifacts for analysis, warns when preserved keylog files are empty, and recreates nginx when needed.

## Module: clean_uninstall (`lib/clean_uninstall/*.sh`)

<a id="fn-clean_all"></a>
### clean_all (`lib/clean_uninstall/clean_all.sh`)
- Entry points: CLI `clean-all`.
- Behavior: runs as a transaction (with rollback on failure), removes a domain from all config/state files, deletes container and attached anonymous volumes and certificates for that domain, clears overrides and security rules for that domain.
- Side effects: edits many files, removes container and anonymous volumes, guarded certificate/path deletions, backups, updates nginx.

<a id="fn-uninstall_all"></a>
### uninstall_all (`lib/clean_uninstall/uninstall_all.sh`)
- Entry points: CLI `uninstall-all`.
- Behavior: supports `--scope backend|config|all` (default `backend`) and always removes Nginx plus backend containers derived from Dockistrate-managed config state. If `nginx-proxy` exists but is not Dockistrate-managed, the command fails before mutating state. Runs as a transaction and uses safe-delete guards for destructive file/dir removals. In `all` scope, `TMP_DIR` removal is deferred until after top-level transaction lock release.
- Scope details: `backend` removes backend-scoped state, generated Nginx config dir, and certificates; `config` removes full config dir and certificates; `all` adds tmp/capture/acme runtime cleanup. Logs and backups are retained across scopes.
- Side effects: deletes containers (with anonymous volumes)/files/certificates and creates a scope-tagged backup record.
- Interactive notes: requires interactive confirmation with exact `YES`; non-interactive invocation fails and tells the operator to re-run as `./dockistrate.sh -i uninstall-all ...`.

## Module: permissions (`lib/permissions/*.sh`)

<a id="fn-fix_permissions_cmd"></a>
### fix_permissions_cmd (`lib/permissions/fix_permissions_cmd.sh`)
- Entry points: CLI `fix-permissions`.
- Inputs: optional `--certbot-darwin-user` or target directory.
- Behavior: runs normal permission repair on the repo root or target directory; with `--certbot-darwin-user`, runs the explicit Darwin Certbot mount preparation path instead.

<a id="fn-fix_permissions"></a>
### fix_permissions (`lib/permissions/fix_permissions.sh`)
- Entry points: `fix-permissions`.
- Behavior: normalizes ownership and modes, preserves executable files, tightens sensitive TLS permissions (keys and PKCS#12 bundles), restricts nginx/certificate directories, and reapplies runtime state hardening policy.
- Side effects: `chown`/`chmod` across repo; may require sudo.

<a id="fn-fix_permissions_certbot_darwin_user"></a>
### fix_permissions_certbot_darwin_user (`lib/permissions/fix_permissions.sh`)
- Entry points: CLI `fix-permissions --certbot-darwin-user`.
- Behavior: on macOS/Darwin under sudo, explicitly prepares the Let's Encrypt and ACME webroot mounts for `SUDO_UID:SUDO_GID` so Certbot can use Darwin host-user mapping; rejects symlinked Certbot mount roots before recursive permission changes.
- Side effects: creates missing Certbot mount roots, changes ownership under real files/directories in those roots without following symlinks, sets directories to `750`, keeps private keys at `600`, and keeps other mounted files owner-writable but restricted.

<a id="fn-_fix_permissions_reject_symlinked_certbot_path"></a>
### _fix_permissions_reject_symlinked_certbot_path (`lib/permissions/fix_permissions.sh`)
- Entry points: `fix_permissions_certbot_darwin_user`.
- Behavior: rejects symlinked Certbot repair roots so the sudo Darwin repair path cannot redirect recursive ownership or mode changes outside the expected mount trees.

<a id="fn-_fix_permissions_prepare_certbot_mount_tree"></a>
### _fix_permissions_prepare_certbot_mount_tree (`lib/permissions/fix_permissions.sh`)
- Entry points: `fix_permissions_certbot_darwin_user`.
- Behavior: prepares one Certbot mount tree with explicit ownership, directory modes, and TLS-aware file modes using non-symlink-following traversal.

<a id="fn-_is_sensitive_tls_file"></a>
### _is_sensitive_tls_file (`lib/permissions/common.sh`)
- Entry points: `fix_permissions`.
- Behavior: detects sensitive TLS files (key files, Let's Encrypt `privkey*.pem`, and PKCS#12 bundles) under the certificate root for special permission handling.

<a id="fn-_portable_stat_mode"></a>
### _portable_stat_mode (`lib/permissions/common.sh`)
- Entry points: TLS permission checks.
- Behavior: reads mode using BSD or GNU stat.

<a id="fn-_ensure_tls_permissions"></a>
### _ensure_tls_permissions (`lib/permissions/common.sh`)
- Entry points: `fix_permissions`.
- Behavior: enforces 640/600 on sensitive TLS files.

<a id="fn-ensure_runtime_state_permissions"></a>
### ensure_runtime_state_permissions (`lib/permissions/common.sh`)
- Entry points: `load_config`, `fix_permissions`.
- Behavior: rejects symlinked runtime state paths, enforces secure-by-default runtime directory/file modes, and tightens existing backup archives/pcap artifacts.

<a id="fn-_print_sudo_hint"></a>
### _print_sudo_hint (`lib/permissions/common.sh`)
- Entry points: `fix_permissions` when not root.
- Behavior: prints a recommended `sudo chown` command.

## Module: tokens (`lib/tokens/*.sh`)

<a id="fn-control_server_tokens"></a>
### control_server_tokens (`lib/tokens/control_server_tokens.sh`)
- Entry points: CLI `control-server-tokens`.
- Behavior: writes managed directive-engine state for `server_tokens` and updates nginx.
- Side effects: updates `state/config/nginx_directives.csv`, removes legacy `server_tokens.conf` include when present, backups, `update_nginx_config`.

<a id="fn-show_server_tokens"></a>
### show_server_tokens (`lib/tokens/show_server_tokens.sh`)
- Entry points: CLI `show-server-tokens`, interactive defaults, `status-all`.
- Behavior: reads managed `server_tokens` directive state and returns `on|off` (`off` default).

<a id="fn-check_config"></a>
### check_config (`lib/tokens/check_config.sh`)
- Entry points: CLI `check-config`.
- Behavior: runs `nginx -t` inside the proxy container.

## Module: nginx_directives (`lib/nginx_directives/*.sh`)

<a id="fn-set_nginx_directive"></a>
### set_nginx_directive (`lib/nginx_directives/set_nginx_directive.sh`)
- Entry points: CLI `set-nginx-directive`.
- Inputs: `scope`, optional `domain`, optional `listen_port`, optional `path_prefix` (for `path` scope), `directive`, `value`.
- Behavior: validates HTTP/stream scope compatibility, validates typed catalog values in scope context, enforces strict ownership policy, upserts directive row, then regenerates nginx config.
- Side effects: writes `state/config/nginx_directives.csv`, backups, `update_nginx_config`.

<a id="fn-set_nginx_directive_raw"></a>
### set_nginx_directive_raw (`lib/nginx_directives/set_nginx_directive_raw.sh`)
- Entry points: CLI `set-nginx-directive-raw`.
- Behavior: validates HTTP/stream scope compatibility plus raw directive/value safety, enforces strict ownership policy, upserts row in `raw` mode, then regenerates nginx config.

<a id="fn-remove_nginx_directive"></a>
### remove_nginx_directive (`lib/nginx_directives/remove_nginx_directive.sh`)
- Entry points: CLI `remove-nginx-directive`.
- Behavior: removes one directive row by `(scope,domain,listen_port,path_prefix,directive)`; in strict mode rejects generic removal of owned directives.

<a id="fn-remove_all_nginx_directives"></a>
### remove_all_nginx_directives (`lib/nginx_directives/remove_all_nginx_directives.sh`)
- Entry points: CLI `remove-all-nginx-directives`.
- Behavior: removes rows by scope/target filters (`all|global|backend|port|path|stream-global|stream-backend|stream-port`); in strict mode skips generic deletion of owned directives.

<a id="fn-list_nginx_directives"></a>
### list_nginx_directives (`lib/nginx_directives/list_nginx_directives.sh`)
- Entry points: CLI `list-nginx-directives`, `status`, `status-all`.
- Behavior: lists directive rows with optional scope/target filters.

<a id="fn-list_nginx_directive_catalog"></a>
### list_nginx_directive_catalog (`lib/nginx_directives/list_nginx_directive_catalog.sh`)
- Entry points: CLI `list-nginx-directive-catalog`.
- Behavior: prints curated typed directive catalog and accepted value formats.

<a id="fn-set_nginx_directive_strict"></a>
### set_nginx_directive_strict (`lib/nginx_directives/set_nginx_directive_strict.sh`)
- Entry points: CLI `set-nginx-directive-strict`.
- Behavior: persists `NGINX_DIRECTIVE_STRICT` (`on|off`) in global settings.

<a id="fn-show_nginx_directive_strict"></a>
### show_nginx_directive_strict (`lib/nginx_directives/show_nginx_directive_strict.sh`)
- Entry points: CLI `show-nginx-directive-strict`.
- Behavior: prints current strict mode value.

<a id="fn-nginx_directives_render_global_include"></a>
### nginx_directives_render_global_include (`lib/nginx_directives/render.sh`)
- Entry points: nginx config generation (`update_nginx_config`).
- Behavior: emits HTTP-context include file for `global` directive rows.

<a id="fn-nginx_directives_render_stream_global_include"></a>
### nginx_directives_render_stream_global_include (`lib/nginx_directives/render.sh`)
- Entry points: nginx config generation (`update_nginx_config`).
- Behavior: emits stream-context include file for `stream-global` directive rows.

<a id="fn-nginx_directives_render_server_directives"></a>
### nginx_directives_render_server_directives (`lib/nginx_directives/render.sh`)
- Entry points: server block generation (`update_nginx_config`, dedicated-host emit).
- Behavior: emits effective server-level overrides with precedence `port > backend > dedicated-host fallback > global`.

<a id="fn-nginx_directives_render_stream_server_directives"></a>
### nginx_directives_render_stream_server_directives (`lib/nginx_directives/render.sh`)
- Entry points: stream server block generation (`update_nginx_config`).
- Behavior: emits effective stream-level overrides with precedence `stream-port > stream-backend > stream-global`.

<a id="fn-nginx_directives_validate_for_render"></a>
### nginx_directives_validate_for_render (`lib/nginx_directives/render.sh`)
- Entry points: nginx update/render preflight.
- Behavior: when strict mode is `on`, fails render if unmanaged owned directives (`server_tokens`, `ssl_protocols`, `ssl_ciphers`) are present in directive state.

## Module: global_settings (`lib/global_settings/*.sh`)

<a id="fn-set_auto_backups"></a>
### set_auto_backups (`lib/global_settings/set_auto_backups.sh`)
- Entry points: CLI `set-auto-backups`.
- Behavior: sets `ENABLE_AUTO_BACKUPS` and saves config.

<a id="fn-set_backup_retention"></a>
### set_backup_retention (`lib/global_settings/set_backup_retention.sh`)
- Entry points: CLI `set-backup-retention`.
- Behavior: sets `BACKUP_RETENTION` (days) and saves config.

<a id="fn-set_backup_compression"></a>
### set_backup_compression (`lib/global_settings/set_backup_compression.sh`)
- Entry points: CLI `set-backup-compression`.
- Behavior: toggles backup compression and saves config.

<a id="fn-set_security_rule_status"></a>
### set_security_rule_status (`lib/global_settings/set_security_rule_status.sh`)
- Entry points: CLI `set-security-rule-status`.
- Behavior: sets global status code for security rule violations and updates nginx.

<a id="fn-set_acl_status"></a>
### set_acl_status (`lib/global_settings/set_acl_status.sh`)
- Entry points: CLI `set-acl-status`.
- Behavior: sets global ACL status code and updates nginx; blocks non-`403` codes when effective ACL policy is `deny` and CIDR allow ACL rules are present.

<a id="fn-set_trusted_proxies"></a>
### set_trusted_proxies (`lib/global_settings/set_trusted_proxies.sh`)
- Entry points: CLI `set-trusted-proxies`.
- Behavior: sets trusted proxy CIDRs and updates nginx.

<a id="fn-set_real_ip_recursive"></a>
### set_real_ip_recursive (`lib/global_settings/set_real_ip_recursive.sh`)
- Entry points: CLI `set-real-ip-recursive`.
- Behavior: toggles `real_ip_recursive`, saves config, updates nginx.

<a id="fn-set_nginx_image"></a>
### set_nginx_image (`lib/global_settings/set_nginx_image.sh`)
- Entry points: CLI `set-nginx-image`.
- Behavior: validates and saves nginx image and pull mode, normalizes tag (tag-less inputs default to `:latest`), recreates nginx unless `SKIP_DOCKER_CHECKS=true`. Default image is `nginx:1.31.1` unless overridden.
- Side effects: writes `GLOBAL_SETTINGS_FILE`, runs `create_nginx_config`, `update_nginx_config`, and `recreate_nginx_container` unless skipped.

<a id="fn-set_nginx_docker_opts"></a>
### set_nginx_docker_opts (`lib/global_settings/set_nginx_docker_opts.sh`)
- Entry points: CLI `set-nginx-docker-opts`.
- Behavior: validates/normalizes nginx docker opts with nginx-profile guardrails, rejects reserved proxy labels under `com.dockistrate.*`, saves `NGINX_DOCKER_OPTS`, and recreates nginx unless `SKIP_DOCKER_CHECKS=true`.
- Side effects: writes `GLOBAL_SETTINGS_FILE`, runs `create_nginx_config`, `update_nginx_config`, and `recreate_nginx_container` unless skipped.

<a id="fn-show_nginx_docker_opts"></a>
### show_nginx_docker_opts (`lib/global_settings/show_nginx_docker_opts.sh`)
- Entry points: CLI `show-nginx-docker-opts`.
- Behavior: prints saved `NGINX_DOCKER_OPTS` or `[None]` when unset.

<a id="fn-set_certbot_image"></a>
### set_certbot_image (`lib/global_settings/set_certbot_image.sh`)
- Entry points: CLI `set-certbot-image`.
- Behavior: validates and saves certbot image and pull mode; tag-less inputs default to `:latest`. Default image is `certbot/certbot:v5.2.1` unless overridden.

## Module: completion (`completion/*.sh`)

<a id="fn-__dockistrate_all_ports"></a>
### __dockistrate_all_ports (`completion/commands/__dockistrate_all_ports.sh`)
- Entry points: bash completion.
- Behavior: Returns all mapped listen ports for completion.

<a id="fn-__dockistrate_backend_domains"></a>
### __dockistrate_backend_domains (`completion/commands/__dockistrate_backend_domains.sh`)
- Entry points: bash completion.
- Behavior: Returns configured backend domains for completion.

<a id="fn-__dockistrate_complete_all_ports"></a>
### __dockistrate_complete_all_ports (`completion/commands/__dockistrate_complete_all_ports.sh`)
- Entry points: bash completion.
- Behavior: Populates COMPREPLY with all known ports.

<a id="fn-__dockistrate_complete_backend_domains"></a>
### __dockistrate_complete_backend_domains (`completion/commands/__dockistrate_complete_backend_domains.sh`)
- Entry points: bash completion.
- Behavior: Populates COMPREPLY with backend domains.

<a id="fn-__dockistrate_complete_ports_for_domain"></a>
### __dockistrate_complete_ports_for_domain (`completion/commands/__dockistrate_complete_ports_for_domain.sh`)
- Entry points: bash completion.
- Behavior: Populates COMPREPLY with ports for a domain.

<a id="fn-__dockistrate_ports_for_domain"></a>
### __dockistrate_ports_for_domain (`completion/commands/__dockistrate_ports_for_domain.sh`)
- Entry points: bash completion.
- Behavior: Returns mapped ports for a given domain.

<a id="fn-_dockistrate_complete"></a>
### _dockistrate_complete (`completion/dockistrate-completion.bash`)
- Entry points: bash completion.
- Behavior: Bash completion entry point; dispatches to command-specific handlers and populates COMPREPLY.

<a id="fn-_dockistrate_complete_backend_domain_commands"></a>
### _dockistrate_complete_backend_domain_commands (`completion/commands/_dockistrate_complete_backend_domain_commands.sh`)
- Entry points: bash completion.
- Behavior: Completes backend domain arguments for domain-aware commands.

<a id="fn-_dockistrate_complete_backend_file_commands"></a>
### _dockistrate_complete_backend_file_commands (`completion/commands/_dockistrate_complete_backend_file_commands.sh`)
- Entry points: bash completion.
- Behavior: Completes domains based on backend override files.

<a id="fn-_dockistrate_complete_headers"></a>
### _dockistrate_complete_headers (`completion/commands/headers.sh`)
- Entry points: bash completion.
- Behavior: Completes header-related arguments and header scopes.

<a id="fn-_dockistrate_complete_misc"></a>
### _dockistrate_complete_misc (`completion/commands/_dockistrate_complete_misc.sh`)
- Entry points: bash completion.
- Behavior: Handles miscellaneous completions such as file paths, `help update`, and `upgrade-preflight --require-backup` plus local tags that match stable `vMAJOR.MINOR.PATCH`.

<a id="fn-_dockistrate_complete_option_values"></a>
### _dockistrate_complete_option_values (`completion/commands/_dockistrate_complete_option_values.sh`)
- Entry points: bash completion.
- Behavior: Completes fixed option values (true/false, allow/deny, etc.).

<a id="fn-_dockistrate_complete_port_domain_commands"></a>
### _dockistrate_complete_port_domain_commands (`completion/commands/_dockistrate_complete_port_domain_commands.sh`)
- Entry points: bash completion.
- Behavior: Completes port arguments for domain-based port commands.

<a id="fn-_dockistrate_complete_port_redirect_options"></a>
### _dockistrate_complete_port_redirect_options (`completion/commands/_dockistrate_complete_port_redirect_options.sh`)
- Entry points: bash completion.
- Behavior: Completes redirect toggle values and status codes.

<a id="fn-_dockistrate_complete_port_tls_commands"></a>
### _dockistrate_complete_port_tls_commands (`completion/commands/_dockistrate_complete_port_tls_commands.sh`)
- Entry points: bash completion.
- Behavior: Completes port arguments for TLS override commands.

## Module: tests (shunit2 framework)

### _shunit_assertSkip (`tests/lib/shunit2.d/_shunit_assertSkip.sh`)
- Entry points: shunit2 test runner.

### _shunit_cleanup (`tests/lib/shunit2.d/_shunit_cleanup.sh`)

- Entry points: shunit2 test runner.

<a id="fn-_shunit_escapeCharInStr"></a>
### _shunit_escapeCharInStr (`tests/lib/shunit2.d/_shunit_escapeCharInStr.sh`)
- Entry points: shunit2 test runner.
- Behavior: Internal: escape a character inside a string.

<a id="fn-_shunit_escapeCharactersInString"></a>
### _shunit_escapeCharactersInString (`tests/lib/shunit2.d/_shunit_escapeCharactersInString.sh`)
- Entry points: shunit2 test runner.
- Behavior: Internal: escape special characters in a string.

<a id="fn-_shunit_execSuite"></a>
### _shunit_execSuite (`tests/lib/shunit2.d/_shunit_execSuite.sh`)
- Entry points: shunit2 test runner.
- Behavior: Internal: execute the registered test suite.

<a id="fn-_shunit_extractTestFunctions"></a>
### _shunit_extractTestFunctions (`tests/lib/shunit2.d/_shunit_extractTestFunctions.sh`)
- Entry points: shunit2 test runner.
- Behavior: Internal: discover test functions in suite files.

<a id="fn-_shunit_fatal"></a>
### _shunit_fatal (`tests/lib/shunit2.d/_shunit_fatal.sh`)
- Entry points: shunit2 test runner.
- Behavior: Internal: print fatal error and exit.

<a id="fn-_shunit_generateReport"></a>
### _shunit_generateReport (`tests/lib/shunit2.d/_shunit_generateReport.sh`)
- Entry points: shunit2 test runner.
- Behavior: Internal: generate the test report summary.

<a id="fn-_shunit_incFailedCount"></a>
### _shunit_incFailedCount (`tests/lib/shunit2.d/_shunit_incFailedCount.sh`)
- Entry points: shunit2 test runner.
- Behavior: Internal: increment the failed assertion counter.

<a id="fn-_shunit_mktempDir"></a>
### _shunit_mktempDir (`tests/lib/shunit2.d/_shunit_mktempDir.sh`)
- Entry points: shunit2 test runner.
- Behavior: Internal: create a temporary directory.

<a id="fn-_shunit_mktempFunc"></a>
### _shunit_mktempFunc (`tests/lib/shunit2.d/_shunit_mktempFunc.sh`)
- Entry points: shunit2 test runner.
- Behavior: Internal: create a temporary function file.

<a id="fn-_shunit_prepForSourcing"></a>
### _shunit_prepForSourcing (`tests/lib/shunit2.d/_shunit_prepForSourcing.sh`)
- Entry points: shunit2 test runner.
- Behavior: Internal: prepare environment before sourcing test files.

<a id="fn-_shunit_shouldSkip"></a>
### _shunit_shouldSkip (`tests/lib/shunit2.d/_shunit_shouldSkip.sh`)
- Entry points: shunit2 test runner.
- Behavior: Internal: check skip state before running assertion.

<a id="fn-_shunit_warn"></a>
### _shunit_warn (`tests/lib/shunit2.d/_shunit_warn.sh`)
- Entry points: shunit2 test runner.
- Behavior: Internal: print a warning message.

<a id="fn-assertContains"></a>
### assertContains (`tests/lib/shunit2.d/assertContains.sh`)
- Entry points: shunit2 test runner.
- Behavior: Assertion helper: string contains expected substring.

<a id="fn-assertEquals"></a>
### assertEquals (`tests/lib/shunit2.d/assertEquals.sh`)
- Entry points: shunit2 test runner.
- Behavior: Assertion helper: values must be equal.

<a id="fn-assertFalse"></a>
### assertFalse (`tests/lib/shunit2.d/assertFalse.sh`)
- Entry points: shunit2 test runner.
- Behavior: Assertion helper: condition must be false.

<a id="fn-assertNotContains"></a>
### assertNotContains (`tests/lib/shunit2.d/assertNotContains.sh`)
- Entry points: shunit2 test runner.
- Behavior: Assertion helper: string does not contain substring.

<a id="fn-assertNotEquals"></a>
### assertNotEquals (`tests/lib/shunit2.d/assertNotEquals.sh`)
- Entry points: shunit2 test runner.
- Behavior: Assertion helper: values must not be equal.

<a id="fn-assertNotNull"></a>
### assertNotNull (`tests/lib/shunit2.d/assertNotNull.sh`)
- Entry points: shunit2 test runner.
- Behavior: Assertion helper: value must be non-empty.

<a id="fn-assertNotSame"></a>
### assertNotSame (`tests/lib/shunit2.d/assertNotSame.sh`)
- Entry points: shunit2 test runner.
- Behavior: Assertion helper: values must differ.

<a id="fn-assertNull"></a>
### assertNull (`tests/lib/shunit2.d/assertNull.sh`)
- Entry points: shunit2 test runner.
- Behavior: Assertion helper: value must be null/empty.

<a id="fn-assertSame"></a>
### assertSame (`tests/lib/shunit2.d/assertSame.sh`)
- Entry points: shunit2 test runner.
- Behavior: Assertion helper: values must match exactly.

<a id="fn-assertTrue"></a>
### assertTrue (`tests/lib/shunit2.d/assertTrue.sh`)
- Entry points: shunit2 test runner.
- Behavior: Assertion helper: condition must be true.

<a id="fn-endSkipping"></a>
### endSkipping (`tests/lib/shunit2.d/endSkipping.sh`)
- Entry points: shunit2 test runner.
- Behavior: Ends a skip block for tests.

<a id="fn-fail"></a>
### fail (`tests/lib/shunit2.d/fail.sh`)
- Entry points: shunit2 test runner.
- Behavior: Forces a test failure with a message.

<a id="fn-failFound"></a>
### failFound (`tests/lib/shunit2.d/failFound.sh`)
- Entry points: shunit2 test runner.
- Behavior: Fails when a value is unexpectedly found.

<a id="fn-failNotEquals"></a>
### failNotEquals (`tests/lib/shunit2.d/failNotEquals.sh`)
- Entry points: shunit2 test runner.
- Behavior: Fails when values are unexpectedly equal.

<a id="fn-failNotFound"></a>
### failNotFound (`tests/lib/shunit2.d/failNotFound.sh`)
- Entry points: shunit2 test runner.
- Behavior: Fails when a value is missing.

<a id="fn-failNotSame"></a>
### failNotSame (`tests/lib/shunit2.d/failNotSame.sh`)
- Entry points: shunit2 test runner.
- Behavior: Fails when values are unexpectedly identical.

<a id="fn-isSkipping"></a>
### isSkipping (`tests/lib/shunit2.d/isSkipping.sh`)
- Entry points: shunit2 test runner.
- Behavior: Reports whether skipping is active.

<a id="fn-startSkipping"></a>
### startSkipping (`tests/lib/shunit2.d/startSkipping.sh`)
- Entry points: shunit2 test runner.
- Behavior: Begins a skip block for tests.

<a id="fn-suite_addTest"></a>
### suite_addTest (`tests/lib/shunit2.d/suite_addTest.sh`)
- Entry points: shunit2 test runner.
- Behavior: Registers a test function with the suite.

## Appendix: Command Call Graphs

Notation: `command -> run_command -> function -> ...`. Function names are linked to their entries; the chain includes all direct calls from the handler in order of first appearance. Most commands call `audit_log`; pre-runtime update commands explicitly skip audit logging. Generated by `scripts/update-function-reference-appendices.sh` using best-effort static parsing.

### Core and Nginx
- `start-nginx` -> [run_command](#fn-run_command) -> [audit_log](#fn-audit_log) -> [start_nginx](#fn-start_nginx) -> [is_valid_image_ref](#fn-is_valid_image_ref) -> [ensure_no_nginx_container_conflict](#fn-ensure_no_nginx_container_conflict) -> [_config_begin_transaction_if_needed](#fn-_config_begin_transaction_if_needed) -> [save_config](#fn-save_config) -> [nginx_container_is_managed](#fn-nginx_container_is_managed) -> [create_nginx_config](#fn-create_nginx_config) -> [update_nginx_config](#fn-update_nginx_config) -> [_nginx_prepare_runtime_rollback](#fn-_nginx_prepare_runtime_rollback) -> [recreate_nginx_container](#fn-recreate_nginx_container) -> [container_running](#fn-container_running) -> [_config_end_transaction_if_started](#fn-_config_end_transaction_if_started) -> [_nginx_release_runtime_rollback](#fn-_nginx_release_runtime_rollback) -> [_rollback_handler](#fn-_rollback_handler)
- `stop-nginx` -> [run_command](#fn-run_command) -> [audit_log](#fn-audit_log) -> [stop_nginx](#fn-stop_nginx) -> [nginx_container_exists_any](#fn-nginx_container_exists_any) -> [require_managed_nginx_container](#fn-require_managed_nginx_container) -> [container_running](#fn-container_running) -> [log_msg](#fn-log_msg)
- `remove-nginx` -> [run_command](#fn-run_command) -> [audit_log](#fn-audit_log) -> [remove_nginx](#fn-remove_nginx) -> [nginx_container_exists_any](#fn-nginx_container_exists_any) -> [require_managed_nginx_container](#fn-require_managed_nginx_container) -> [remove_container_and_anonymous_volumes](#fn-remove_container_and_anonymous_volumes) -> [log_msg](#fn-log_msg)
- `status` -> [run_command](#fn-run_command) -> [audit_log](#fn-audit_log) -> [status](#fn-status) -> [_status_run](#fn-_status_run)
- `status-all` -> [run_command](#fn-run_command) -> [audit_log](#fn-audit_log) -> [status_all](#fn-status_all) -> [_status_run](#fn-_status_run)
- `fix-default-config` -> [run_command](#fn-run_command) -> [audit_log](#fn-audit_log) -> [fix_default_config](#fn-fix_default_config) -> [runtime_state_path_guard_if_declared](#fn-runtime_state_path_guard_if_declared) -> [validate_tls_settings_for_render](#fn-validate_tls_settings_for_render) -> [runtime_state_paths_guard_if_declared](#fn-runtime_state_paths_guard_if_declared) -> [state_backend_ports_parse_line](#fn-state_backend_ports_parse_line) -> [get_port_http3_state](#fn-get_port_http3_state)

## Appendix: Full Command Call Chains

Notation: `command -> run_command -> optional audit_log -> handler -> transitive calls`. Transitive calls list unique repo-defined functions reachable from the handler in breadth-first order; conditional branches, dynamic dispatch, and source may not execute at runtime. If `recreate_nginx_container` appears through `update_nginx_config`, that path is conditional: `update_nginx_config` reloads when possible and recreates only when required (for example, port-binding or container-state changes). Generated by `scripts/update-function-reference-appendices.sh`.
